title: Combinatorics
summary: Provides factorial, Pochhammer-type, and rational combinatorial identities used by the coefficient proof.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Provides factorial, Pochhammer-type, and rational combinatorial identities used by the coefficient proof.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal boundary
mathematicalContent.body: This page describes exactly the declarations present in PolyGJMSoperators/OR/Coefficients/Combinatorics.lean; claims not represented by a declaration are not counted as verified.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: factorial Q. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Definition: binom Q. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Definition: multinomial Q3. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def multinomialQ3 (N a b c : ℕ) : ℚ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: pochhammer Q. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Definition: falling Factorial Q. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Formal proposition: factorial Q zero. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Formal proposition: factorial Q succ. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem factorialQ_succ (m : ℕ) : factorialQ (m + 1) = ((m + 1 : ℕ) : ℚ) * factorialQ m

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: binom Q zero right. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Formal proposition: binom Q self. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem binomQ_self (m : ℕ) : binomQ m m = 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: pochhammer Q zero. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Formal proposition: pochhammer Q succ. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem pochhammerQ_succ (x : ℚ) (m : ℕ) : pochhammerQ x (m + 1) = pochhammerQ x m * (x + (m : ℚ))

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: falling Factorial Q zero. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Formal proposition: falling Factorial Q succ. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem fallingFactorialQ_succ (x : ℚ) (m : ℕ) : fallingFactorialQ x (m + 1) = fallingFactorialQ x m * (x - (m : ℚ))

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: pochhammer Q eq pred mul. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem pochhammerQ_eq_pred_mul (x : ℚ) {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m) : pochhammerQ x m = pochhammerQ x (m - 1) * (x + ((m - 1 : ℕ) : ℚ))

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: falling Factorial Q eq pred mul. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem fallingFactorialQ_eq_pred_mul (x : ℚ) {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m) : fallingFactorialQ x m = fallingFactorialQ x (m - 1) * (x - ((m - 1 : ℕ) : ℚ))

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
